title: Designing AI resistant technical evaluations
summary: What we learned from three iterations of a performance engineering take-home that Claude keeps beating.
guidance: Evaluating technical candidates becomes harder as AI capabilities improve.

I've now iterated through three versions of our take-home in an attempt to ensure it still carries signal. Each time, I’ve learned something new about what makes evaluations robust to AI assistance and what doesn't.
Each time, I’ve learned something new about what makes evaluations robust to AI assistance and what doesn't.
A take-home that distinguishes well between human skill levels today may be trivially solved by models tomorrow—rendering it useless for evaluation.
Tristan designed—and redesigned—the take-home test that's helped Anthropic hire dozens of performance engineers.

We needed a way to evaluate candidates more efficiently. So, I took two weeks to design a take-home test that could adequately capture the demands of the role and identify the most capable applicants.

actions.rationale: Sync learnings into a retrieval-first, non-constitutional document.
Potential retrieval and compaction guidance; requires governed review before changing runtime behavior.
Potential frontend quality guidance; keep proposal-only until independently validated.
Potential eval coverage change; requires explicit validation and proposal review.
Potential specialist coverage update; keep proposal-only until the need is validated.
